items.title: In Sinaloa's capital, news of a boxing scion's arrest and allegations of cartel ties cause unease
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>In Mexico's northwestern city of Culiacan, young boxers honored a legend in the ring—while outside, a statue of Julio César Chávez stood tall. But the man himself was absent, as his family faced a turbulent week marked by his son's immigration arrest in Los Angeles. 

(AP video by Aarón Ibarra)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Rescue efforts are underway near Camp Mystic as Texas experiences mass flooding
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Rescuers were searching Saturday for more than two dozen children from a girls’ camp and many others who are still missing after a wall of water blasted down a river in the Texas Hill Country. (AP video by Julio Cortez)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Officials: At least 27 people are dead in Texas floods as the search continues for the missing
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A Texas sheriff says the deaths from flash flooding in central Texas has risen to 27 people, including nine children. Rescuers are continuing the search Saturday for children from a girls' camp and many others who were still missing. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: China puts aircraft carrier on display in Hong Kong
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Shandong is China's second aircraft carrier and has recently been taking part in exercises in the Pacific. China's first aircraft carrier, called Liaoning, came to Hong Kong in 2017.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A Texas sheriff says the deaths from flash flooding in central Texas has risen to 27 people, including nine children. (AP video shot by: Lekan Oyekanmi)

items.title: Animal rights protest against San Fermin bull runs in Spain
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Wearing horns and fake blood, they marched through the city in northern Spain to demonstrate against the controversial sport. The nine-day event, will be watched by thousands in the city and broadcast on live television.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Vast area destroyed in Rome after petrol station blast that injured more than 40
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Two among those injured were reported to be in “severe condition,” and hospitalized at Rome's Casilino hospital. Eleven of the injured are from law enforcement corps — police and carabinieri — and one is a firefighter, they said, adding they were not in life-threatening conditions.

(Produced by Maria Grazia Murru)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: River Seine reopens for swimming in Paris
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Swimming in the Seine had been illegal since 1923, with a few exceptions, due to pollution and risks posed by river navigation. Taking a dip outside bathing areas is still banned for safety reasons.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: One of world's biggest Legolands opens to tourists in Shanghai
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Built with 85 million Lego bricks, it was developed by Merlin Entertainments and the LEGO Group in partnership with the Shanghai government. The park features replicas of real world sights like Beijing’s Temple of Heaven. Tickets range from US$44 to US$84.

(AP video by Olivia Zhang)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Dalai Lama attends prayer in Dharamshala a day ahead of his 90th birthday
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>His followers see him as the face of Tibet’s aspirations for greater autonomy, but have for years wrestled with the idea that he might be the last person to hold the role. He put that speculation to rest Wednesday, just days before he turns 90 on Sunday. There will be a successor after his death, he announced, and the Dalai Lama’s office will lead the search and recognize a successor in accordance with past tradition.

(AP video by Rishi Lekhi and Shonal Ganguly)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Coffins of Liverpool footballer Jota and brother leave church after funeral in Portugal
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The coffins of Liverpool forward Diogo Jota and his brother André Silva were taken from the Igreja Matriz de Gondomar church for burial after their funeral in Portugal on Saturday. Liverpool players joined family and friends for the funeral of their teammate Jota and his brother, two days after the siblings died in a car crash in Spain.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Congregation flees after arsonist sets fire to an Australian synagogue door
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A wave of antisemitic attacks has roiled Australia since the on Oct. 7, 2023, Hamas assault on Israel triggered the war in Gaza. Jewish and Muslim organizations and hate researchers have recorded drastic spikes in hate-fueled incidents on both groups.


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: River Seine in Paris reopens for public swimming for first time in a century
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>For the first time in over a century, Parisians and tourists will be able to take a refreshing dip in the River Seine. The long-polluted waterway is finally opening up as a summertime swim spot following a 1.4 billion euro ($1.5 billion) clean-up project that made it suitable for Olympic competitions last year.

(AP video by Alexander Turnbull, produced by Tom Nouvian)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Coffins of Liverpool footballer Jota and his brother leave chapel of rest for funeral in Portugal
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The coffins of Liverpool forward Diogo Jota and his brother André Silva were taken from a chapel of rest to the nearby Igreja Matriz de Gondomar church for their funeral in Portugal on Saturday. Their bodies were brought to the chapel for a wake on Friday, a day after the Portuguese soccer players were killed in a car crash.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: LIVE: Texas officials give update on flash flooding
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Watch live as Texas Division of Emergency Management officials hold a press conference. Multiple deaths have been reported in Texas after heavy rain caused flash flooding.</article>
